reasoning: The Carnegie Global Protest Tracker typically records distinct waves of "significant antigovernment protests", rather than every discrete protest event. Since the 2024-2025 South Korean political crisis was triggered by the December 2024 martial law declaration, the tracker will almost certainly use a December 2024 start date for this protest wave. As the question was launched in early 2025, any entry representing this movement will fall outside the resolution criteria (which strictly requires a start date after the question's launch). The base rate of a completely new, distinct mass anti-government movement (10,000+ participants) breaking out in the short 4-5 month period before July 2025, and being distinctively added as a new entry to the tracker, is quite low given the tracker averages less than a hundred entries per year globally. I estimate a 12% chance for a new, tracker-eligible protest starting in this tight timeframe.
